title: Phase 2 — Per-finding triage classifier (noise / fyi / decision)
body:
## Summary

Phase **2** of the wizard work: classify every finding into one of three classes — **noise / fyi / decision** — so the wizard can focus the human on what they actually need to decide and roll the rest into an audit count.

Stacked on top of PR #34 (the wizard slice). Once #34 lands, this rebases to trunk automatically.

## Rules (defaults)

| When                                         | Class    |
|----------------------------------------------|----------|
| dismissed                                    | noise    |
| severity ∈ {high, critical}                  | decision |
| severity = medium AND certainty = detected   | decision |
| severity = medium                            | fyi      |
| severity = low AND certainty = detected      | fyi      |
| severity = low                               | noise    |
| unknown / missing severity                   | decision (fail safe) |

These are intentionally hardcoded for Phase 2 — Phase 2.5 can lift them into `GuardianConfig` once usage tells us where the boundaries should sit.

## What changed

**`src/pr_guardian/decision/finding_triage.py`** *(new)*

- `classify_finding(finding) -> "noise" | "fyi" | "decision"` — pure function, takes a JSON-shaped finding dict.
- `tag_findings_with_triage(agent_results)` — in-place mutation that adds a `triage` field to every finding, returns a counts roll-up.

**`src/pr_guardian/api/dashboard.py`**

Calls `tag_findings_with_triage` in the review-detail endpoint right after dismi
